query: ok so we're switching from openai text-embedding-3-large to something else for our internal docs search since the new pricing tier is killing us. heard voyage might be cheaper. is voyage-3.5 still the latest or is there a v4? our corpus is mostly english product docs and slack threads.

we have ~200k legal contracts in an s3 bucket (pdfs, mostly NDAs and master service agreements) and want to do retrieval over them with pinecone. our current embedding model is jina-v3 but the retrieval is mediocre on legal language. what would you recommend

im embedding python source code from a monorepo (about 800 files, ~80k LOC across packages). running it through pinecone for code search. which voyage model and what dim should i use? worried about storage costs

im seeing weird retrieval results from my pinecone index. top hits look semantically related but miss exact identifier matches like fn parse_config. using voyage-code-3 with input_type=document on upsert. what am i doing wrong
voyage api keeps returning 429s when i hit it with larger batches. im sending ~200 docs per request. whats the recommended batch size and do they have a paid tier with higher limits

i have a pinecone index that uses llama-text-embed-v2 as an integrated model. i want to switch the embedding model to voyage-code-3 (it's a code corpus). is that even possible without rebuilding the index? if not whats the safest migration
my coworker mentioned matryoshka embeddings yesterday and i nodded along but i have no idea what those are. she was talking about cutting our pinecone storage. does voyage support this
ive been embedding both my docs and my queries with input_type=None because i wasnt sure if the parameter mattered. is that bad? im using voyage-3.5 against a 50k record pinecone index
do voyage embeddings work with cosine or dot product on pinecone, or does it matter? im setting up a new index

whats the difference between voyage-3.5 and voyage-4-lite? building a customer support search bot with maybe 30k articles, expect modest query volume. cost matters but not at the expense of bad results
how do i query my pinecone integrated index using the MCP tool? i want to find docs related to 'oauth setup' across my project-docs index
set up sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 locally for embedding our internal docs without sending data to a third party. python codebase
whats the best openai embedding model for code? we use text-embedding-3-large currently but i heard 3-small might be enough
i want to build a vector search using huggingface BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 against a chromadb instance. python
create a new pinecone serverless index called 'product-feedback' on aws us-east-1, dim 1536, metric cosine
can you explain dot product vs cosine similarity to me? what should i pick for normalized embeddings vs unnormalized
how do i upsert vectors to pinecone with the python sdk in batches? im using openai embeddings already
im fine-tuning a sentence transformer on our internal product docs to improve retrieval. has anyone else done this and what learning rate worked
implement BM25 retrieval in elasticsearch for our product search. we're moving off algolia
whats the input format for openai's /v1/embeddings endpoint? need to make sure i pass it correctly
